# {{< i18n risk_register >}} {#sec-risk-register}


## Purpose of This Document

This Risk Register provides the **complete and authoritative list of project
risks**, including:

- Risk identification and categorization
- Probability and impact assessment
- Risk exposure level
- Ownership
- Associated KPIs and Stage‑Gates

This document is a **mandatory PMBOK artifact** and is the primary input to the
**Risk Response Plan**.

---

## Risk Scoring Methodology

### Probability Scale

- Low (L)
- Medium (M)
- High (H)

### Impact Scale

- Low (L)
- Medium (M)
- High (H)
- Very High (VH) — reserved for ecological risk

### Risk Exposure

Derived qualitatively from Probability × Impact and used for prioritization:

- Low
- Medium
- High
- **Critical** (ecological priority)

---

## Detailed Risk Register (Full Table)

| ID | Description | Cat. | Prob. | Impact | Exposure | Primary KPI | Stage-Gate | Risk Owner | Notes |
|----|-------------------------------|-----|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|------------|------------|------|
| R1 | Automated screening incorrectly excludes benthic‑relevant imagery (false negatives) | Environmental / Ecological | M | **VH** | **Critical** | KPI‑3 | G1, G2, G3, G4 | Environmental SME Lead | Primary ecological risk |
| R2 | Annotation subjectivity leads to inconsistent ecological labeling | Environmental / Data | H | H | High | KPI‑4 | G1, G2 | Environmental SME Lead | Requires consensus process |
| R3 | Model fails to meet minimum ecological performance thresholds | Technical | M | H | High | KPI‑3 | G2 | Data Science Lead | MVP risk accepted within limits |
| R4 | Severe class imbalance biases model behavior | Data | H | M | High | KPI‑3 | G2 | Data Science Lead | Expected, mitigable |
| R5 | Poor image quality (turbidity, lighting, backscatter) reduces screening reliability | Data / Operational | H | M | High | KPI‑3 | G2, G3 | Data Engineering Lead | Environmental conditions driven |
| R6 | Delays in environmental SME availability | Operational | M | H | High | KPI‑2 | G1, G2 | Project Manager | Direct schedule risk |
| R7 | Misalignment between technical and ecological expectations | Governance | M | H | High | KPI‑3 | G1, G2 | Project Manager | Requires frequent checkpoints |
| R8 | Over‑automation reduces expert trust in outputs | Operational / Adoption | M | M | Medium | KPI‑1 | G3 | Product Owner | Human‑in‑the‑loop critical |
| R9 | Stakeholder resistance to adopting AI‑assisted workflows | Organizational | M | M | Medium | KPI‑6 | G3 | Product Owner | Change management risk |
| R10 | Licensing or framework compliance issues | Compliance | L | H | Medium | — | G1 | IT / Legal | Preventive control |
| R11 | Insufficient compute or storage resources | Technical / Infra | L | M | Low | KPI‑2 | G1 | IT / Infra | Mitigable |
| R12 | Loss of traceability or auditability of datasets | Governance / Data | L | H | Medium | KPI‑5 | G3, G4 | Data Governance | Impacts reuse |
| R13 | Inability to generalize across fields | Technical / Ecological | M | H | High | KPI‑4 | G3 | Data Science Lead | Blocks scaling |
| R14 | Scope creep toward non‑MVP objectives | Governance | M | M | Medium | — | All | Project Manager | Controlled by WBS |
| R15 | Environmental credibility questioned due to undocumented limitations | Ecological / Governance | L | **VH** | **Critical** | KPI‑3 | G2, G3 | Environmental SME Lead | Documentation mandatory |

---

## Risk Prioritization Summary

### Critical Risks (Must Never Be Accepted)

- R1 — Ecological data loss (false negatives)
- R15 — Environmental credibility loss

### High Risks (Must Be Mitigated)

- R2, R3, R4, R5, R6, R7, R13

### Medium Risks (Managed / Monitored)

- R8, R9, R10, R12, R14

### Low Risks

- R11

---

## Governance Rules

- **Any Critical risk automatically blocks Stage‑Gate progression**
- Ecological risks override schedule and cost considerations
- Risk ownership is mandatory and non‑delegable
- All risk status changes must be logged

---

## Relationship to Other Risk Artifacts

| Artifact | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| Risk Register | Risk identification & prioritization |
| Risk Response Plan | Actions and contingencies |
| Stage‑Gates | Decision enforcement |
| KPI Framework | Continuous risk monitoring |

---

## Review Cadence

- Weekly review by Project Manager
- Mandatory review at every Stage‑Gate
- Immediate escalation if KPI‑3 degrades

---

## Baseline and Change Control

This Risk Register is **baselined** upon approval.  
Any addition, removal, or reclassification of risks requires formal change
control.
